name: JetBrains MCP Server Plugin
what_it_does: Official JetBrains MCP Server Plugin enabling AI agents to interact with JetBrains IDEs (IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, WebStorm, etc.) — reading and editing files, running code, accessing project structure, executing terminal commands, and integrating JetBrains IDE capabilities into agent-driven development workflows.
use_cases: Reading and editing source files within JetBrains IDEs from coding agents
Executing code and tests via IDE run configurations from development agents
Accessing project structure and refactoring tools from code analysis agents
Running terminal commands within IDE from automation agents
AI pair programming within IntelliJ/PyCharm/WebStorm from coding agents
Integrating JetBrains AI Assistant with external agent workflows
not_for: VS Code or other editors — JetBrains IDEs only
Headless CI/CD environments without IDE running
Teams using command-line development workflows without IDE
best_when: A developer using JetBrains IDEs needs AI agent integration — official plugin provides deep IDE integration with access to all JetBrains platform features.
avoid_when: You use VS Code, Cursor, Zed, or other editors — use their respective MCP integrations.

auth.notes: No authentication — local MCP server running inside JetBrains IDE as plugin. IDE must be running. Official JetBrains plugin.

pricing.free_tier_limits: IntelliJ IDEA Community Edition is free; Plugin is free
pricing.notes: Plugin is free. Some JetBrains IDEs require paid license (PyCharm Pro, WebStorm, etc.). Community editions are free.

performance.rate_limits: Local IDE — no external rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: JetBrains IDE must be running with project open — specific prerequisite
Plugin installed via JetBrains Marketplace — install step required
IDE version compatibility — plugin may require specific IDE version range
Code execution uses IDE run configurations — agents must understand run config setup
File paths are project-relative — agents must use correct path conventions
Some operations require user confirmation in IDE — may interrupt automated flows
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local IDE execution. No credentials for MCP. Official JetBrains plugin — trusted source. IDE has full project access. Terminal execution can access system resources.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local IDE execution. Official JetBrains plugin. Full project access — trusted environment. No external data exposure.
